req.title: Biller registration
req.statement: A customer shall register a biller only after a 24-hour hold; withdrawals to new billers shall be deferred.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-023 mandates a 24-hour hold before biller activation and defers withdrawals to new billers. The test suite validates: (1) successful biller registration enters HOLD state via positive path, (2) duplicate biller registration returns 409 via negative path, (3) biller registration at exactly 24-hour boundary transitions to ACTIVE via boundary analysis, and (4) state transitions from HOLD→ACTIVE via STC. Tests are deterministic by mocking time, idempotent via unique biller identifiers, and traceable to the requirement statement.

tests.name: test_biller_registration_success_enters_hold_state
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
User account exists and is active
Biller does not already exist in system
actions: POST /billers with valid biller details (name, routing_number, account_number)
expected: Response hold_until field is 24 hours from current timestamp
Biller record persists in database with status HOLD
tests.executable:
def test_biller_registration_success_enters_hold_state():
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {<valid_jwt_token>}"}
    payload = {
        "biller_name": "Test Utility Co",
        "routing_number": "021000021",
        "account_number": "123456789"
    }
    resp = <requests_post>("http://localhost/billers", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    data = resp.json()
    assert "biller_id" in data
    assert data["biller_status"] == "HOLD"
    assert "hold_until" in data
    assert <extract_hours_from_timestamp>(data["hold_until"]) == 24

tests.name: test_duplicate_biller_registration_returns_409
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
User account exists and is active
Biller with identical routing_number and account_number already registered
actions: POST /billers with biller details matching existing biller
Verify response status and error details
expected: Response contains error_message field with human-readable message
trace: R-FIN-023: error pathway - 409 if duplicate biller

tests.name: test_biller_hold_period_boundary_24_hours
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
User account exists and is active
System clock is mocked to controllable state
Biller registered 24 hours ago enters HOLD state
actions: Register biller at time T0, confirm status HOLD
Advance mock time by exactly 24 hours to T0+24h
Verify biller eligibility for withdrawal transitions to ACTIVE
expected: Withdrawals to biller are allowed at T0+24h and onward
trace: R-FIN-023: state transition HOLD→ACTIVE after hold expires
R-FIN-023: withdrawals deferred until hold expires
tests.executable:
def test_biller_hold_period_boundary_24_hours():
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {<valid_jwt_token>}"}
    with <freeze_time>("2024-01-01 12:00:00"):
        payload = {
            "biller_name": "Time Test Biller",
            "routing_number": "021000021",
            "account_number": "999999999"
        }
        resp = <requests_post>("http://localhost/billers", json=payload, headers=headers)
        biller_id = resp.json()["biller_id"]
        assert resp.json()["biller_status"] == "HOLD"
    with <freeze_time>("2024-01-02 12:00:00"):
        resp = <requests_get>(f"http://localhost/billers/{biller_id}", headers=headers)
        assert resp.json()["biller_status"] == "ACTIVE"

tests.name: test_withdrawal_to_hold_biller_deferred
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
Biller registered and in HOLD state
Current time is within 24-hour hold period
actions: POST /withdrawals with target_biller pointing to HOLD biller
expected: Withdrawal is not created or queued for immediate processing
Withdrawal request is rejected (not deferred silently)
trace: R-FIN-023: withdrawals to new billers shall be deferred
R-FIN-023: hold state prevents withdrawal processing
tests.executable:
def test_withdrawal_to_hold_biller_deferred():
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {<valid_jwt_token>}"}
    biller_resp = <requests_post>("http://localhost/billers",
        json={"biller_name": "Hold Test", "routing_number": "021000021",
        "account_number": "555555555"}, headers=headers)
    biller_id = biller_resp.json()["biller_id"]
    withdraw_resp = <requests_post>("http://localhost/withdrawals",
        json={"amount": 100.00, "biller_id": biller_id}, headers=headers)
    assert withdraw_resp.status_code == 422
    assert withdraw_resp.json()["error_code"] == "BILLER_ON_HOLD"

tests.name: test_biller_state_transition_hold_to_active
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
actions: Verify initial state is HOLD with hold_until set
expected: State transition is irreversible (no revert to HOLD)
hold_until field becomes null or deprecated in ACTIVE state
trace: R-FIN-023: biller becomes eligible for withdrawals post-transition
tests.executable:
def test_biller_state_transition_hold_to_active():
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {<valid_jwt_token>}"}
    with <freeze_time>("2024-01-01 10:00:00"):
        resp = <requests_post>("http://localhost/billers",
            json={"biller_name": "STC Test", "routing_number": "021000021",
            "account_number": "777777777"}, headers=headers)
        biller_id = resp.json()["biller_id"]
        assert resp.json()["biller_status"] == "HOLD"
    with <freeze_time>("2024-01-02 10:00:01"):
        resp = <requests_get>(f"http://localhost/billers/{biller_id}", headers=headers)
        assert resp.json()["biller_status"] == "ACTIVE"
        assert resp.json().get("hold_until") is None
